data.title: Distributed Capital Partner Reveals Details of 2022 Crypto Theft
data.description:
On March 30, Distributed Capital Partner Shen Bo disclosed details of his stolen assets from 2022 on X. According to BlockBeats, the theft occurred between 00:46 and 01:02 Eastern Time on November 10, 2022. Shen Bo's Trust Wallet hot wallet, installed on an iPhone 12 Pro Max, was compromised, resulting in the loss of approximately $42 million in assets. The addresses involved in the theft are as follows:

ETH: 0x6be85603322df6DC66163eF5f82A9c6ffBC5e894

BTC: 1ECNeZyiHgqJmv42i3pkWY48xiXy7KukTG / bc1qg3mnvn8saea50js7nzkhm8k054mpwqmcuq3de5

TRON: TJLBmmUb5TcFFXTLzuuaKU96uTg5Sjn1yD

On March 26, Shen Bo announced a bounty to recover the stolen crypto assets, valued at approximately $42 million, which were taken three years ago.

data.title: Ether Faces Potential 40% Decline Amid Weak Accumulation and ETF Outflows
data.description: Analyst Leshka.eth has warned that Ether could experience a significant drop of 40% to $1,200 in the coming weeks if it fails to maintain the $1,990 support level. According to NS3.AI, Glassnode data indicates a lack of strong accumulation among major Ether holders, contributing to the bearish outlook. Additionally, U.S. spot Ether ETFs have recorded approximately $300 million in net outflows during the same period, further exacerbating concerns about Ether's market stability.

data.title: STOCKS | Mixed Performance in China's A-Share Market as Indices Fluctuate
data.description:
China's A-share market experienced a mixed performance on March 30, with indices showing varied results. According to Jin10, the Shanghai Composite Index rose by 0.24%, while the Shenzhen Component Index fell by 0.25%, and the ChiNext Index declined by 0.68% by the close of trading. The combined trading volume of the Shanghai and Shenzhen markets reached 1.92 trillion yuan, an increase of 62.6 billion yuan from the previous trading day.

In terms of sector performance, the commercial aerospace concept saw significant gains, with stocks like Aerospace Power and Shenjian Co. hitting their daily limit. The innovative drug sector remained active throughout the day, with companies such as Dongcheng Pharmaceutical and Lianhuan Pharmaceutical also reaching their daily limit. Aluminum stocks surged, with multiple stocks including Tianshan Aluminum hitting their daily limit. The optical communication sector strengthened in the afternoon, with stocks like Changfei Fiber and Hengtong Optic-Electric reaching their daily limit.

Additionally, sectors such as grain, gold, oil, media and entertainment, and food and beverage recorded notable gains. Conversely, sectors including electricity, insurance, diversified finance, and hotel and catering experienced declines. Overall, the market saw more gainers than losers, with approximately 2,900 stocks rising across the board.

data.title: Anti-War Protest in Tel Aviv Draws Over 1,000 Amid Declining Support for Conflict
data.description: More than 1,000 individuals participated in an anti-war protest in Tel Aviv on the 28th, as support for the ongoing conflict in Israel shows signs of waning. According to NS3.AI, Israeli sources indicate a significant drop in the number of people who strongly support the war since early March. The protest saw participation from numerous peace organizations, including Partners for Peace Plan, who rallied against what they described as an 'endless war.'

data.title: Telegram Experiences Suspected Outage on March 30
data.description: On March 30, Telegram users reported potential disruptions on the platform. According to BlockBeats, data from the network monitoring site DownDetector indicated a suspected outage affecting Telegram services. Users experienced difficulties accessing the platform, leading to widespread reports of service interruptions. The cause of the outage remains unclear, and Telegram has yet to release an official statement addressing the issue.

data.title: Support for the Iraq War is declining, and anti-war protests have erupted in several parts of Israel.
data.description: According to Israeli sources, on the 29th local time, as the military operation against Iran continued, public support for the war in Israel declined, especially the proportion of people who "strongly support" the war, which has dropped significantly compared to early March. In recent days, dozens of peace organizations, including the Israeli "Peace Partnership" program, have joined forces for the first time to protest the Israeli government's "endless war." The report indicates that an anti-war protest in Habima Square in Tel Aviv on the 28th drew over 1,000 participants, the largest in a month. The crowd held signs reading "Eternal war is not strategy" and "Even in such complex times, we must oppose (war)." The event was originally scheduled to last one hour, but actually extended to nearly two hours due to clashes with police. (CCTV News)

data.title: Binance to Update RWUSD Subscription Options with United Stables
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, a significant update for RWUSD will take effect on 2026-03-30. Users in eligible jurisdictions will be able to subscribe to RWUSD using U (United Stables) at a 1:1 ratio. This update is in addition to the existing options of subscribing with USDT or USDC, depending on regional eligibility.

RWUSD is described as a principal-protected yield product offering flexible-term subscriptions with daily APR rewards. It is designed to provide stable returns in both rising and falling market conditions. The yield is supported by income generated from Binance’s ecosystem and other sources, including real-world assets like tokenized US Treasury Bills. Users can subscribe to RWUSD through the USD Yield Hub by entering the subscription amount, agreeing to the terms, and confirming the subscription.

Qualified global users can subscribe to RWUSD with USDT and U, while eligible users in the EEA region can use USDC. For redemption, Binance will return assets to USDC from RWUSD on a 1:1 ratio, regardless of the initial subscribed assets. Fast Redemption incurs a 0.1% fee, while Standard Redemption incurs a 0.05% fee. Occasionally, Binance may offer a daily free Fast Redemption quota, subject to its discretion. The APR is discretionary and subject to change, representing an estimate of rewards in cryptocurrency over the selected timeframe. RWUSD is not a stablecoin, security, fund, tokenized RWA, or on-chain asset, and it does not represent any user rights to RWAs. It is not available to U.S. persons and cannot be traded, transferred, or withdrawn on-chain.

data.title: Swiss Economic Indicator Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: The Swiss KOF Economic Leading Indicator for March registered at 96.1, falling below the anticipated 100.8, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from 104.2 to 103.8, indicating a downward trend in economic expectations.

data.title: Chicago Wheat Prices Rise Amid Iran Conflict and Weather Concerns
data.description:
Chicago wheat prices have surged as geopolitical tensions in Iran escalate, coupled with adverse weather conditions affecting global supply. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has raised concerns over potential disruptions in wheat exports from the region. Additionally, unfavorable weather patterns are threatening crop yields, further contributing to the price increase.

Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation, as the combination of geopolitical instability and weather-related risks could lead to significant fluctuations in wheat prices. The conflict in Iran has heightened fears of supply chain disruptions, while unpredictable weather conditions continue to pose challenges for agricultural production.

Traders and investors are advised to remain vigilant, as the evolving situation may influence market dynamics in the coming weeks. The interplay between geopolitical events and environmental factors underscores the complexity of predicting commodity prices, particularly in the agricultural sector.

data.title: White House App Raises Privacy Concerns Amid Tracking Capabilities
data.description: The launch of a new app by the White House has sparked privacy concerns among users and security researchers. According to NS3.AI, one developer has claimed that the app can track a phone every 4.5 minutes when in the foreground and every 9.5 minutes in the background, provided permission is granted. The app's privacy policy indicates that it automatically stores IP address information, and names and email addresses may be retained for subscribers.

data.title: Fenbushi Capital's Shen Bo Reveals $42 Million Asset Theft
data.description: Shen Bo of Fenbushi Capital has disclosed a significant asset theft amounting to $42 million that took place on November 10, 2022. According to NS3.AI, the theft involved a Trust Wallet hot wallet and targeted victim addresses across Ethereum, Bitcoin, and TRON networks.

data.title: STOCKS | South Africa's Benchmark Index Faces Worst Month in Two Decades Amid Dual Crises
data.description:
South Africa's benchmark stock index is experiencing its worst monthly performance in nearly two decades, according to Jin10. The dual impact of the Iran conflict has weakened demand for emerging market assets, while a sharp decline in precious metal prices has pressured the country's mining stocks. As of the close on March 27, the FTSE/JSE All Share Index has fallen 13% this month, marking the steepest drop since the peak of the global financial crisis in September 2008. Previously, the index had recorded 12 consecutive months of gains until February, setting a historic record for the longest streak of increases. The current situation represents a dramatic reversal.

The precious metals and mining sector, which accounts for a quarter of the index's weight, has plummeted 27% since the onset of the Middle East conflict, erasing all gains made earlier this year as gold and platinum prices decline. Meanwhile, emerging market stocks have faced widespread sell-offs, with investors concerned that soaring oil prices will drive inflation higher, forcing central banks to raise interest rates. SBG Securities analysts Deanne Gordon and Adele Fermoyle noted in a report that profit-taking in precious metals counters due to risk aversion has amplified the impact on South Africa's stock market.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank May Signal April Rate Hike Amid Middle East Uncertainty, Economists Say
data.description: Economists from BNP Paribas have indicated that despite significant uncertainty in the Middle East, the summary of opinions from the Bank of Japan's March meeting suggests a potential rate hike in April. According to Jin10, they noted that in the current economic environment, the extent to which rising import costs are passed on to prices is quite significant. As a result, many policy committee members may feel it necessary to prioritize the risk of upward inflation expectations. They added that if tensions in the Middle East ease in the coming weeks and crude oil prices stabilize to some extent, thereby reducing economic downside risks, the Bank of Japan may find it easier to shift towards a rate hike at the April meeting.

data.title: Capital B Raises €2.8 Million to Support Bitcoin Acquisition Strategy
data.description: Capital B has successfully raised €2.8 million ($3.22 million) by converting convertible bonds into common stock and selling additional new shares. According to NS3.AI, the company stated that the funds will be used to bolster its Bitcoin acquisition strategy.

data.title: Doubao Integrates with Douyin E-commerce for Seamless In-App Purchases
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). Doubao, a ByteDance application, has integrated with Douyin's e-commerce platform, allowing users to complete product orders and payments directly within the Doubao app without needing to switch to Douyin. This feature is currently in the testing phase, according to reports from the Science and Technology Innovation Board Daily.

data.title: Spot gold rose more than 1.00% on the day, currently trading at $4,540.49 per ounce.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold rose more than 1.00% on the day, currently trading at $4,540.49 per ounce.

data.title: The yield on 40-year Japanese government bonds rose 10 basis points to 4.020%.
data.description: The yield on 40-year Japanese government bonds rose 10 basis points to 4.020%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Polymarket's accumulated fee revenue has exceeded $15 million, essentially offsetting LP subsidy expenses.
data.description: According to data compiled by Gate Research on Dune, Polymarket has accumulated over $15.18 million in transaction fees since it began charging transaction fees on some markets on January 6. Meanwhile, Polymarket has reportedly paid out $15.36 million in subsidies to liquidity providers (LPs), meaning the platform's fee revenue has essentially offset its total liquidity subsidy expenditures.

data.title: Rising Demand for Electricians Amid AI Advancements in Texas
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). In Texas, the demand for electricians is surging, with young professionals in data centers earning annual salaries between $240,000 and $260,000. This trend is notable as these individuals avoid the burden of student loans and college tuition fees. Meanwhile, the number of entry-level programming positions is decreasing as AI technology accelerates coding processes, causing concern among white-collar workers about potential job displacement.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has emphasized the need for AI factories, but the initial workforce required for these facilities is not engineers, but electricians. Mike Rowe, speaking on Fox Business, highlighted a shift in societal perceptions, noting that schools continue to portray blue-collar jobs as a fallback option. However, the reality is changing, with white-collar workers worried about AI replacement, while electricians face an abundance of job opportunities.

data.title: Australia and India to Engage in Upcoming Trade Discussions
data.description: Australia and India are set to engage in trade discussions in the coming weeks, according to Trade Minister Don Farrell. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these talks as both nations aim to strengthen their economic ties. The discussions are expected to cover a range of trade-related issues, reflecting the growing partnership between the two countries. This meeting comes at a time when both Australia and India are seeking to enhance their trade relationships globally.
